description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and sharply defined, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible openings or additional features. The coloration is predominantly dark gray to black, with the grooved areas appearing slightly lighter due to surface texture or material variation. The block has a substantial, heavy look, suggesting it is made from dense stone or concrete. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a traffic divider or protective curb.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The block is dark gray to black and appears solid and robust.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, with the parallel stripes clearly visible and evenly spaced across the top.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the depth and spacing of the grooves, showing the lighter color within the grooves against the darker block.
description_view_2: The block is viewed from the opposite grooved side, with the striped pattern again prominent and the block's thickness visible.
description_view_3: The grooves are clearly visible from this angle, with the alternating light and dark stripes creating a strong visual contrast.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street median as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or placed directly on a prepared surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-defined, with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
